title: Reference
text: This page renders the current CLI help by running the ir executable on PATH during the Quarto render.

title: Reference
text:
ir


Run self-describing R scripts

Usage: ir [COMMAND]

Commands:
  run    Run a script or inline R expression
  tool   Run package executables
  cache  Manage ir's cache
  help   Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help     Print help
  -V, --version  Print version

title: Reference
text:
ir run


Run a script or inline R expression

Usage: ir run [OPTIONS] [ARGS]...

Arguments:
  [ARGS]...  Rscript options, script path, and script arguments

Options:
  -e, --expr <EXPR>       Evaluate an inline R expression instead of a script file
      --with <PKG>        Add a dependency for this run; may be repeated
      --r-version <SPEC>  Select the R version for this run with rig
      --isolated          Disable the user library for this run
  -h, --help              Print help

title: Reference
text:
ir tool


Run package executables

Usage: ir tool [COMMAND]

Commands:
  run      Resolve a package and run an executable from its exec directory
  install  Install package executable launchers
  help     Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help  Print help

title: Reference
text:
ir tool run


Resolve a package and run an executable from its exec directory

Usage: ir tool run [OPTIONS] [ARGS]...

Arguments:
  [ARGS]...  Rscript options, package ref or command, and tool arguments

Options:
      --from <PKG_REF>    Resolve a package ref and run <command> from its exec/ directory
      --with <PKG>        Add a dependency for this tool run; may be repeated
      --r-version <SPEC>  Select the R version for this tool run with rig
  -h, --help              Print help

title: Reference
text:
ir tool install


Install package executable launchers

Usage: ir tool install [OPTIONS] <PKG_REF>

Arguments:
  <PKG_REF>  Package ref that resolves to the package exposing exec/ launchers

Options:
      --with <PKG>        Add a dependency for installed launchers; may be repeated
      --r-version <SPEC>  Select the R version for installed launchers with rig
      --bin-dir <DIR>     Directory where launchers are written
      --force             Overwrite an existing launcher path
  -h, --help              Print help

title: Reference
text:
ir cache


Manage ir's cache

Usage: ir cache [COMMAND]

Commands:
  clean  Clear the cache, removing all entries
  dir    Show the cache directory
  help   Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
  -h, --help  Print help

title: Reference
text:
ir cache dir


Show the cache directory

Usage: ir cache dir

Options:
  -h, --help  Print help

title: Reference
text:
ir cache clean


Clear the cache, removing all entries

Usage: ir cache clean [OPTIONS]

Options:
      --force  Force removal of the cache
  -h, --help   Print help

title: Package Tools
text:
ir tool run resolves a package and runs an executable from that package’s exec/ directory.
For ir tool run --from pkg tool, ir installs pkg, finds exec/tool or exec/tool.R, and launches it with the selected Rscript. A bare self-named package ref such as ir tool run btw is shorthand for ir tool run --from btw btw.
Tool runs support the same dependency and R selection flags as script runs:
Package refs use pak syntax. Quote refs when the shell would otherwise interpret characters such as >.

title: Package Tools
text:
Install launchers
ir tool install resolves a package, scans that package’s exec/ directory, and writes launchers for the executables it finds.
$ ir tool install btw
$ ir tool install github::r-lib/Rapp
$ ir tool install --bin-dir ~/.local/bin btw
$ ir tool install --force btw
Installed launchers keep the same resolved package ref, dependency additions, and R version selection that were used at install time.

title: Scripts
text:
ir run executes R scripts whose dependency metadata is embedded at the top of the file. The metadata block is YAML, with each line prefixed by #| after an optional shebang.
Run the file through ir:

title: Scripts
text:
Dependency metadata
The script frontmatter supports:

dependencies: package refs resolved with pak.
r-version: an optional R version requirement selected from installed versions reported by rig.
exclude-newer: an optional YYYY-MM-DD date that resolves CRAN from the Posit Package Manager snapshot for that date.

Supported version specs include pkg, pkg>=1.0, and pkg==1.2. Other pak refs, including GitHub refs and URL refs, pass through unchanged.

title: Scripts
text:
Inline runs
Inline expressions use the same resolver and cache:
$ ir run -e '1 + 1'
$ ir run --with cli -e 'cli::cli_alert_success("works")'
$ ir run --with 'dplyr>=1.1' --with tidyr -e 'library(dplyr); library(tidyr); 1'
--with may be repeated and accepts comma-separated packages. With a script, command-line packages are merged with the script metadata; with -e, they are the only declared dependencies.

title: Scripts
text:
Isolation
By default, ir prepends the resolved library to .libPaths() and leaves user libraries available as a fallback. Use --isolated to remove the user library for the run:
$ ir run --isolated script.R
$ ir run --isolated --with cli -e 'cli::cli_alert_success("hi")'

title: ir
text: ir runs standalone R scripts and Quarto documents that declare their runtime requirements in the file itself. It resolves those requirements into cached package libraries and runs the program with the selected R.

title: ir
text:
Features

Declare dependencies in script frontmatter and run the file directly with ir run.
Resolve packages once, then reuse content-addressed cached libraries on later runs.
Pin CRAN resolution to a Posit Package Manager snapshot with exclude-newer.
Select installed R versions with --r-version or frontmatter r-version.
Evaluate inline expressions with ir run -e and add command-line dependencies with --with.
Render Quarto documents through ir run report.qmd using the same dependency metadata model.
Run package-shipped executables from an installed package’s exec/ directory with ir tool run.
Install persistent launchers for package executables with ir tool install.
Inspect and clear the cache with ir cache dir and ir cache clean.

title: ir
text:
Common commands
$ ir run script.R
$ ir run --with cli -e 'cli::cli_alert_success("hi")'
$ ir run --r-version 4.5 report.qmd --to html
$ ir tool run --from btw btw --help
$ ir tool install btw
$ ir cache dir

title: Quarto Documents
text:
ir run renders .qmd and .Rmd files with quarto render. Dependencies live under an ir: key in the document YAML.
---
title: "My report"
ir:
  dependencies:
    - dplyr>=1.0
    - gt@1.0
  r-version: ">= 4.0"
  exclude-newer: "2024-01-15"
---
Run the document with the same command used for scripts:
$ ir run report.qmd
$ ir run report.qmd --to pdf
$ ir run --vanilla report.qmd
ir resolves the document dependencies into the same cached library model used for scripts, then launches quarto render. Leading Rscript options are forwarded to Quarto’s knitr engine. Trailing arguments are passed to quarto render.
When an R version is selected by frontmatter or --r-version, ir pins Quarto’s knitr R to that selection.

title: Cache
text:
ir caches both dependency resolution and materialised package libraries.
Resolution requests are keyed by declared dependencies, resolution source, R version, and platform. If the same request was already resolved, ir reuses the cached library and does not invoke pak again. Latest resolution includes the current date in the key, so newly published versions are picked up at most once per day. Snapshot resolution uses the exclude-newer date.
Materialised libraries are content-addressed by the resolved package set, R version, and platform. Scripts or tools that resolve to the same set share one library.

title: Cache
text:
Commands
$ ir cache dir
$ ir cache clean
ir cache dir prints the cache root. ir cache clean removes the whole ir cache, including materialised libraries and resolution markers.
